mnemonics: IVF - Initialize Variables First: Remember to set up your initial state (I) before any rendering or logic (V for View, F for Function) happens.
keyPoints: Initial state is the default data structure of a component before any user interaction or data fetching.
It is defined in the constructor for class components or via the `useState` hook in functional components.
A well-defined initial state prevents runtime errors and ensures the component renders correctly on first load.
commonMistakes: Mutating state directly instead of using `setState` or the state setter function.
Forgetting to provide a complete initial state, leading to 'undefined' errors when trying to access properties.
Defining initial state with props that will change, instead of using it only for default, self-contained values.
